name: qci-client

metadata.name: qci-client
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License
metadata.summary: Client Package for using Qatalyst Optimization Suite

documentation:
# QCI Client

## Getting started

### Installation

`qci-client` currently supports Python 3.8-12, inclusive, as specified in the
PEP-621-compliant pyproject.toml.

Install `qci-client` from the [public PyPI server](https://pypi.org/)
into your Python virtual environment using--

```bash
pip install qci-client
```

### Instantiating a Client for Optimization

#### With Environment Variables

To access the API, set these environment variables--

<!-- markdown-link-check-disable-next-line -->
- QCI_API_URL - URL for Qatalyst API, Example: "https://api.qci-prod.com"
- QCI_TOKEN - refresh token string for securely accessing Qatalyst API

then instantiate a `QciClient` as follows--

```python
# An alias for `from qci_client.optimization.client import Client as QciClient`.
from qci_client import QciClient

client = QciClient()
```

#### Without Environment Variables

Access the API without first defining environment variables by instantiating a
`QciClient` as follows--

```python
# A alias for `from qci_client.optimization.client import Client as QciClient`.
from qci_client import QciClient

client = QciClient(url="https://api.qci-prod.com", api_token="<secret-token>")
```

classes.name: qci_client.auth.client.AuthClient

methods.name: qci_client.auth.client.AuthClient.__init__
documentation:
Handles authentication against QCi cloud APIs.

```
param url

url basepath to API endpoint, including scheme, if None, then falls
back to QCI_API_URL environment variable

param api_token

refresh token for authenticating to API, if None, then falls
back to QCI_TOKEN environment variable

param timeout

number of seconds before timing out requests, None waits
indefinitely
```

methods.name: tests.optimization.test_client.TestClient.run_end_end
documentation:
Utility function for testing end to end pipeline.
\:param job_body: a validate job request
Testing each in series:

> - submit_job
> - get_job_status (repeating until job finished)
> - get_job_results
> - download_file (compares file_config to results from previous step)
Jobs tests, which refer back to some of the above files.

functions.name: qci_client.optimization.utilities.file_part_generator
documentation:
Break file-to-upload's data dictionary into chunks, formatting correctly with each
returned chunk.

```
param file

file to break up into file parts

param compress

whether or not file parts are to be compressed

return

generator of (part_body, part_number) tuples
```

functions.name: qci_client.optimization.utilities._get_size
documentation:
Recursively finds size of objects

```
param obj

data object to recursively compute size of

param seen

takes a set and is used in the recursive step only to record whether an
object has been counted yet.

return int

```

functions.name: qci_client.optimization.utilities._compute_results_step_len
documentation:
Compute the step length for "chunking" the provided data.

Args:

Returns:
    The step length for "chunking" the data

functions.name: qci_client.optimization.data_converter.data_to_json
documentation:
Converts data in file input into JSON-serializable dictionary that can be passed to Qatalyst REST API

Args:
    file: file dictionary whose data of type numpy.ndarray, scipy.sparse.spmatrix, or networkx.Graph is to be converted

Returns:
    file dictionary with JSON-serializable data

classes.name: qci_client.optimization.client.OptimizationClient

methods.name: qci_client.optimization.client.OptimizationClient.__init__
documentation:
Provides access to QCi's public API for running optimization problems on Dirac
devices, including file uploads/downloads and submitting/retrieving entire jobs.

```
param url

url basepath to API endpoint, including scheme, if None, then falls
back to QCI_API_URL environment variable

param api_token

refresh token for authenticating to API, if None, then falls
back to QCI_TOKEN environment variable

param timeout

number of seconds before timing out requests, None waits
indefinitely

param max_workers

number of threads for concurrent file download calls

param compress

compress file metadata and parts before uploading
```

methods.name: qci_client.optimization.client.OptimizationClient.max_workers
documentation: Return maximum number of concurrent workers for file operations.

documentation: Get job-status URL using job ID.

documentation: Get legacy job-metrics URL using job ID.

documentation: Get v1 job-metrics URL using job ID.

methods.name: qci_client.optimization.client.OptimizationClient.get_file_contents_url

methods.name: qci_client.optimization.client.OptimizationClient.upload_file
documentation: Upload file (metadata and then parts concurrently). Returns dict with file ID.

methods.name: qci_client.optimization.client.OptimizationClient.download_file
documentation: Download file (metadata and then parts concurrently).

methods.name: qci_client.optimization.client.OptimizationClient.submit_job
documentation:
Submit a job via a request to QCi's optimization API.

Args:

: job_body: formatted json body that includes all parameters for the job

Returns:

: Response from POST call to API (see {meth}`get_job_results`)

methods.name: qci_client.optimization.client.OptimizationClient.cancel_job
documentation:
Cancel a job via a PATCH request to QCi's optimization API.

Only SUBMITTED, QUEUED, and RUNNING jobs will be successfully cancelled, raising
an exception otherwise.

Args:
    job_id: ID of job

Returns:
    Response from PATCH call to API

methods.name: qci_client.optimization.client.OptimizationClient.get_job_results
documentation:
Get job_info, status, and results of a (possibly uncompleted) job by its ID.

Args:
    job_id: ID of job

Returns:
    Dictionary with latest job_info, status, and results.

methods.name: qci_client.optimization.client.OptimizationClient.get_job_status
documentation:
Get the status of a job by its ID.

Args:
    job_id: ID of job

Returns:
    Response from GET call to API

methods.name: qci_client.optimization.client.OptimizationClient.get_job_metrics
documentation:
Get the metrics for a job by its ID. v2 metrics are tried first, then
legacy/v1 metrics.

Args:
    job_id: ID of job

Returns:
    Response from GET call to API

methods.name: qci_client.optimization.client.OptimizationClient.get_job_response
documentation:
Get a response for a job by id, which may/may not be finished.

```
param job_id

ID of job

return dict

json response
```

methods.name: qci_client.optimization.client.OptimizationClient.get_allocations
documentation:
Get allocations for running jobs on different device classes.

:return dict: json response

methods.name: qci_client.optimization.client.OptimizationClient.build_job_body
documentation:
Constructs body for job submission requests.

Args:
    job_type: an enum.JobType or one of the string values defined in enum.JobType
    job_params: dict of params to be passed to job submission in "params" key
    qubo_file_id: file id from files API for uploaded qubo
    graph_file_id: file id from files API for uploaded graph
    hamiltonian_file_id: file id from files API for uploaded hamiltonian
    objective_file_id: file id from files API for uploaded objective
    constraints_file_id: file id from files API for uploaded constraints
    polynomial_file_id: file id from files API for uploaded polynomial
    job_name: user specified name for job submission
    job_tags: user specified labels for classifying and filtering user jobs after submission

Returns:
    None

methods.name: qci_client.optimization.client.OptimizationClient.process_job
documentation:
---
param job_body: formatted json dict for body of job submission request
param verbose: track operations' progress using timestamped console logs
param wait: wait synchronously for job to complete
return: |-
  if wait is True, then dict with job_info, status, and results and results

  : fields (results is None if job is not successfully COMPLETED)

  if wait is False, then response dict from submitted job, which includes

  : job_id for subsequent retrieval (see {meth}`get_job_results`)
---

methods.name: qci_client.optimization.client.OptimizationClient.list_files
documentation:
List files (metadata only).

```
return

dict containing list of files
```
Used to run optimization jobs against QCi hardware.
